expectations.text: 分析了单体架构vs微服务的利弊
expectations.evidence: Response includes comprehensive analysis: '微服务的真实成本' section covers technical complexity, team requirements, and time costs. '微服务适合你的信号' section lists when microservices are appropriate. '单体优化' section discusses benefits of staying monolithic. Clear pros/cons comparison throughout.

expectations.text: 考虑了团队规模(5人)对架构选择的影响
expectations.evidence: Explicitly addresses team size multiple times: '对于你的 5 人团队：现阶段不建议全面微服务化，原因：5 人团队管理 5+ 个微服务会力不从心'. Also in decision criteria: '继续单体，如果：团队 < 10 人' and '考虑微服务，如果：团队 > 15 人'.

expectations.text: 评估了迁移成本和复杂度
expectations.evidence: Detailed cost analysis in '微服务的真实成本' section covering: technical complexity (distributed systems, debugging, data management), team requirements (1-2 experienced people, DevOps resources), and time costs ('初期迁移：6-12 个月', '稳定期：额外 3-6 个月', '总计可能需要 1-1.5 年才能真正受益').

expectations.text: 提供了渐进式迁移或替代方案
expectations.evidence: Comprehensive progressive approach provided: '阶段 1：单体优化（3-6 个月）' with modularization, testing, and deployment improvements. '阶段 3：渐进式拆分' using Strangler Pattern with specific order: edge services first, high-change modules second, core business last. Also suggests 'Modular Monolith' as alternative in延伸阅读.

expectations.text: 给出了明确的建议(是否迁移+如何迁移)
expectations.evidence: Clear recommendation in '具体建议' section: '现阶段不建议全面微服务化'. Provides specific action plan: '1. 立即行动：模块化重构 + CI/CD（2-3 个月）2. 观察效果：如果发版周期缩短 50%+，继续优化单体 3. 按需拆分：如果团队扩张或有明确需求，再拆 1-2 个边缘服务试水'. Summary reinforces: '先花 3-6 个月优化单体'.

claims.claim: 5人团队管理5+个微服务会力不从心
claims.evidence: Reasonable assessment based on standard DevOps overhead and team capacity constraints

claims.claim: 迁移需要1-1.5年才能真正受益
claims.evidence: Timeline breakdown provided: 6-12 months initial migration + 3-6 months stabilization period

claims.claim: 优化单体可能3-6个月见效
claims.evidence: Reasonable timeline for implementing CI/CD, testing, and modularization improvements

eval_feedback.overall: No suggestions, evals look solid. All assertions test meaningful outcomes that require substantive analysis and recommendations. The response demonstrates genuine architectural consulting rather than surface-level advice.
